Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 証拠保管室前／警備室
English: February 23 Evidence Room Entrance Guard Station

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。この、きわめて シュミ性の強い空間は‥‥
English: What's with the decor in this place? It's very... eccentric.

Japanese: パンフレットによると、 《保管室前・警備室》ですけど。
English: According to the pamphlet, this is the guard station for the evidence room.

Japanese: じゃあ、あのドアの向こうが 証拠保管室‥‥“殺人現場”か。
English: So, beyond that door is the evidence room... the scene of the crime?

Japanese: そういうことになりますね。 ‥‥‥‥‥うううう‥‥‥‥‥
English: It sure seems that way... Oh. Ohhh...

Japanese: ど。どうしたの？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: ヨコのサボテンくんの存在感に圧倒 されて、考えがまとまりません！
English: It's those cacti! They're so prickly... so imposing! It's hard to think straight.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (If you can't handle the cacti, stay out of the desert...)

Japanese: そもそも、警備室なのに、 どうして警備のヒトがいないんだ！
English: What I want to know is, if this is a guard station, where is the guard?

Japanese: “警備のヒト”‥‥ だいたい、想像はつきますね。
English: I have a feeling I know who this guard is already...

Japanese: かなり、チクチクしますね。 ‥‥ホンモノかな、コレ。
English: Yipes, that sure is prickly. It must be the real deal.

Japanese: このデカいのが１コあれば、 じゅうぶんだと思うけど。
English: I would think just one big one would be sufficient.

Japanese: この、サボテン。 ‥‥お姉ちゃん、みたいですね。
English: This cactus... is a lot like my sister, actually.

Japanese: と、ともえさん‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Skye?

Japanese: 冷たくてカタいカラをかぶって、 さらにトゲで身を守って‥‥
English: Encased in a cold, rigid shell, with thorns pointing in every direction...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、そっくり。
English: Just like her.

Japanese: 悪いけど、どう見ても ともえさんには似てないよ。
English: You know, I've been looking at this cactus a while now, and I don't see the resemblance.

Japanese: あたしも、フンイキで 言っちゃいました。
English: It's more an attitude thing than a physical similarity.

Japanese: 証拠保管室の映像が 映し出されているみたいだ。
English: It looks like there's a video feed from the evidence room here.

Japanese: モニターの下に、 ランプが光ってますよ。
English: There's a light blinking below the monitor.

Japanese: 《録画中》‥‥って 書いてあります！
English: It says "Recording"!

Japanese: このパソコンで、出入りするヒトを チェックしてたみたいですね！
English: I bet we could use this computer to check on who went in and out of here!

Japanese: 警備員さんの服が つるしてあります！
English: There's a security guard uniform hanging here.

Japanese: “制服”じゃないコトは たしかだね。
English: It looks more like a costume than a uniform, honestly.

Japanese: カワのジャンパーに、 カワのズボンに、カワの‥‥
English: A leather jacket, leather pants, a leather...

Japanese: あれ。これ、なんでしたっけ！
English: What was that called again?

Japanese: パンチ‥‥ピンチ‥‥ペンチ‥‥ あ、ポンチ！
English: A punchy? A paunchy? A pinchy? I know! A poochy!

Japanese: ううん‥‥今ひとつ ピンと来ないなあ‥‥
English: Hmm... Wait, maybe that wasn't it.

Japanese: （“ポンチョ”だけど、もう少し ダマっておこう‥‥）
English: (It's a "poncho," but I think I'll keep that information to myself for the time being.)

Japanese: こんなところに、 投げナワがありますよ。
English: Look, on the floor, a lasso!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、“ワナ”っぽい ムードがただよってるなあ。
English: Hmm... looks like it's set up to trap something.

Japanese: ワナっぽい‥‥ねえ‥‥ あ！　できました！
English: A trap, here? Wait, I know!

Japanese: 《“ワナげ”な投げナワ》 ‥‥逆から読んでも同じです！
English: Maybe someone was trying to catch a wild bull in here... But the lasso missed!

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、スゴイね。
English: You sure have an active imagination.

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: さあ、ハリきって行きましょう！
English: Let's just walk in!

Japanese: 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: そりゃ、そうだろうね。
English: You thought it'd be open?

Japanese: （現場を調べるには、 だれかの協力が必要だな‥‥）
English: (I think we'd need someone's permission to go in there first...)

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: ガント局長さんに ＩＤカードも借りてきたし。
English: And we have the ID card from Chief Gant!

Japanese: さあ、ハリきって行きましょう！
English: Let's just walk in!

Japanese: 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: あ！　カード端末の 電源がオフになってるぞ。
English: Ah hah! The card reader is turned off, see?

Japanese: もう！　警備のヒト、 ちゃんとシゴトしましょうよ！
English: What is that security guard thinking?

Japanese: わあ。西部のサルーンみたい！ この、スイングドア。
English: This swinging door makes the place look like some kind of saloon!

Japanese: でも‥‥クギで打ちつけてあるぞ。 入れないよ、これじゃ。
English: But look, it's nailed shut. You can't get in that way.

Japanese: だってホラ。ここから入ったら、 サボテンが倒れてきて‥‥
English: Of course not! If you went in through here...

Japanese: チクッ、てなりますから。
English: The cactus would fall over. Ouch!

Japanese: ‥‥“チクッ”じゃ すまないぞ、きっと。
English: I'd say it'd be more of an "Yeeeeaargh," myself.

Japanese: おやおや‥‥バンビーナが ごきげんナナメのようだよ。
English: Howdy, pardners. Well, well, what's made my bambina's skies so gray?

Japanese: ざ、罪門さん！ （やっぱり、このヒトか‥‥）
English: O-Officer Marshall! (Somehow, I knew...)

Japanese: なんだい？　その“やっぱり、 このヒトか‥‥”みたいな目は。
English: What's that "somehow I knew" look for?

Japanese: ‥‥お察しのとおり。 ここはオレのサルーンさ。
English: As you may have surmised, this here's my saloon.

Japanese: あの‥‥あたしたち、 現場を調査したいんですけど。
English: Um... we're here to investigate the crime scene.

Japanese: ほお‥‥そのムネのカード。 新世界へのティケットかい。
English: Yeehaw! That card you got there on your chest.
That's better than a sheriff's badge in these parts.

Japanese: てぃけっと‥‥
English: Y-yeehaw?

Japanese: いいさ。サツジン現場で 開拓者気分を味わってくることさ。
English: Well, what ya standin' there for? Get along, little dogies. The crime scene's a waiting!

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: （カードリーダーの 電源がもどったみたいだな）
English: (Looks like the card reader's on again.)

Japanese: ‥‥ついでに、お話を 聞かせていただけますか？
English: While we're here, I was wondering if we could ask you some questions?

Japanese: 悪いがカウボーイ。 キミたちの相手をする気はないさ。
English: Sorry, cowboy, but I got no mind to tangle with you hombres.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥忙しいんですか？
English: You're... busy, then?

Japanese: そんなコトは言ってないだろう？
English: Did I say that?

Japanese: “相手をする気はない”‥‥ それだけのことさ。
English: I only said I didn't wish to speak with you.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Actually, you said you had "no mind to tangle with us hombres.")

Japanese: かなり、チクチクしますね。 ‥‥ホンモノかな、コレ。
English: This cactus sure is prickly. It must be real.

Japanese: ビリーに近づかないほうがいい。 ‥‥ケガ、するぜ。
English: Best keep your distance from Billy there. You're liable to get hurt.

Japanese: びりー‥‥ このサボテンのコトですか？
English: Billy... The cactus has a name?

Japanese: 左ウデに、カウボーイハットを かぶってますね。ビリー。
English: Billy, you're wearing your cowboy hat on your hand.

Japanese: ああ‥‥、そのカウスキンの ローハイドかい。
English: Ah, my rawhide cow skin 10-gallon hat!

Japanese: 買ったばかりだったんだけど。 ビリーが気に入ったみたいでね。
English: I just picked that up, but Billy took a fancy to it.

Japanese: （‥‥トゲに引っかかって とれなくなったみたいだな）
English: (Looks like it got stuck on the thorns and he couldn't get it off.)

Japanese: ずいぶんいっぱい モニターがありますね！
English: You sure have a lot of monitors in here!

Japanese: 証拠保管室は、ゼンブで ８つ、あるからね。
English: There's eight cameras in the evidence room in all.

Japanese: １部屋に１つ、防犯カメラが 設置されているのさ。
English: One for each section.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。事件があった保管室の モニターは、どれですか？
English: Which monitor shows the section where the crime took place?

Japanese: そいつは‥‥“神のみぞ知る” ってヤツだな。
English: Why do cattle sit before the rains come? It's not for man to know.

Japanese: ‥‥警備員シッカクですね、 罪門さん。
English: Unless you're the security guard responsible!

Japanese: カワのジャンパーに、 カワのズボンに、カワの‥‥
English: A leather jacket, leather pants, and a leather...

Japanese: ズボンじゃないさ。チャップスに ‥‥そっちは、ポンチョだね。
English: Those aren't pants, those are chaps. And that's a poncho up there.

Japanese: あ、それ！　ポンチョ！
English: Ah hah! A poncho!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいけど、 名前はかわいらしいですね。
English: It sure looks cool, but the name is a little silly.

Japanese: こんなところに、 投げナワがありますよ。
English: Look, on the floor, it's a lasso.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、“ワナ”っぽい ムードがただよってるなあ。
English: Hmm... looks like it's set up to trap something.

Japanese: さっき、デカいウシを ひっとらえたぜ‥‥そのワナで。
English: I just roped me a steer with this here lasso.

Japanese: え！
English: What!?

Japanese: “もーッス！”“もーッス！” ‥‥しきりに、鳴いてたっけな。
English: It was a' bellowing "moo, pal! moo!"

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事か‥‥）
English: (Detective Gumshoe, I presume...)

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: カードリーダーの電源は 入れておいたぜ。
English: I turned on the card reader.

Japanese: せいぜい、楽しんでくることさ、 カウボーイ！
English: Go have yourself a ball, pardners!

Japanese: このＩＤカード、 あたしが借りたんですからね！
English: And I have the ID card! A real ID card!

Japanese: （‥‥早いトコ、現場を 調べたほうがいいかな‥‥）
English: (Let's get this investigation started already...)

Japanese: わあ。西部のサルーンみたい！ この、スイングドア。
English: Look at this swinging door! Just a like a wild west saloon!

Japanese: おっと‥‥。 今日はもう、閉店だぜ。
English: Ah... Sorry, pardner, but we're closed for the day.
Huh?

Japanese: 早く帰ってくれねえかい、 アンタたち。
English: Y'all get along home now!

Japanese: お店のマスター気取りですね、 罪門さん。
English: I think Officer Marshall really wanted to be a wild west bartender.

Japanese: ‥‥一度、このセリフが 言ってみたくてね。
English: I always wanted to say that.

Japanese: この先に、あたしたちの 新世界が広がっているワケですね！
English: Beyond that door, a whole new world awaits us!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人現場の証拠保管室、だね。
English: Right, the evidence room where the murder took place...

Japanese: 新世界へのティケットなら、 ここにありますし。
English: And our ticket to this new world is right here.

Japanese: ‥‥局長さんから借りた お客さん用のＩＤ、だね。
English: The visitor's ID we borrowed from the Chief of Police I'm guessing...

Japanese: さあ。いざ！ このサルーンを出発しましょう！
English: Right! Let's leave this saloon behind us in the dust!

Japanese: （‥‥罪門巡査から、よくない 影響をモロに受けているな‥‥）
English: (She's definitely the impressionable type...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。おハナシを うかがいたいんですけど‥‥
English: Um, I was wondering if we could talk to you...

Japanese: 悪いが、バンビーナ。
English: Sorry, bambina.

Japanese: オレは今、タンブル・ウィードと ともに、荒野をかけめぐってるよ。
English: But I'm off to roam the lands, like a tumbleweed on the wide prairie.

Japanese: ガンスリンガーが、マスケットに 弾丸をこめるよう、祈りながらね。
English: Like a gunslinger loading his six-shooter, I say a little prayer.

Japanese: ‥ぐぎゅるるぐぐ‥‥ぅぅ‥‥う‥
English: GRROOOOORRK

Japanese: ‥‥で。ケッキョク、どういう コトですか？　成歩堂さん！
English: What was that all about, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ハラをすかせて、しゃべる気分じゃ ない。‥‥そういうコト、かな。
English: I think he was just too hungry to talk.

Japanese: それ‥‥、最後の“音”だけで キメつけてませんか？
English: You're just saying that because his stomach was growling!
You have no idea what he was talking about either!

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 なんとかしないとな‥‥）
English: (Well, in any case, we need to get cracking on this investigation, pronto!)

Japanese: 罪門さん。証拠保管室の 警備を担当してるんですよね？
English: Officer Marshall, you're in charge of security for the evidence room, right?

Japanese: ダレにでもできる、ありがたい シゴトさ。‥‥よく知ってるね。
English: You got good eyes, pardner. It's an easy job, and I'm grateful for it.

Japanese: 留置所の、原灰巡査から 教えてもらいました。
English: Actually, Officer Meekins at the detention center told us.

Japanese: ああ‥‥あの、 カワイソウなボウヤか。
English: Ah, that poor little dogie?

Japanese: ２回に１回は、自分の名前を “灰原”と書きまちがえる‥‥
English: Poor guy, I keep getting his name wrong and calling him "Meekly."

Japanese: 彼は‥‥こんなコトも 教えてくれました。
English: He told us something.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、あなたは 警備室にいなかった‥‥と。
English: He said that, when the stabbing occurred... you weren't at your station.

Japanese: こんなコト、言っちゃマズいが。
English: Well, maybe I shouldn't be telling you this...

Japanese: 今から、２年前。 捜査官からハズれてから‥‥
English: But since I got demoted from detective two years ago...

Japanese: 見た目ほど、ヤル気マンマン ってワケじゃないんだよ。
English: Well, it might not look it, but I lost my fire for the job, you know?

Japanese: あの‥‥何をしてたんですか？ 事件が起こった、５時１５分ごろ。
English: So... what were you doing around 5:15 when the murder took place?

Japanese: さあね‥‥。ジップ・コチーズ号で 街道をギャロップで流してたかな。
English: Well...
I reckon I was galloping down the highway on the back of my steed, Zippy.

Japanese: ‥‥名馬にまたがって 高速道路を走っていた、と。
English: Note: he was riding down the highway on his horse named "Zippy."

Japanese: とりあえず、ここには ニンゲンは必要ないのさ。
English: There's no need for people here, anyhow.

Japanese: 最新のキカイたちが、キチンと 見張ってくれるらしいからね。
English: These newfangled machines do a bang-up job of keeping an eye on the place.

Japanese: 最新のキカイ‥‥ “防犯システム”ですか。
English: You mean the security camera system?

Japanese: キカイ‥‥か。
English: I don't take to machines much.

Japanese: ステーキのヨコの、ゆでた ブロッコリーの次にニガテだよ。
English: Kinda like that stewed broccoli they sneak in next to your steak, you know?

Japanese: おキョウさんから聞きました。
English: Ms. Starr told us something...

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥２年前まで 捜査官だったんだ、って。
English: She said that you were a detective until two years ago.

Japanese: “事件現場のブッシュワッカー” ‥‥荒野にうずめたユメのアトさ。
English: It was always my dream to be a rawhide wrangler on the scene of the crime...
That's all gone now... Like a drinking hole in a prairie fire.

Japanese: 今も、調べてるんですよね。 ＳＬ９号事件‥‥おキョウさんと。
English: You're still investigating the SL-9 Incident with Ms. Starr, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥あれは、オレの事件だった。
English: That was my case...

Japanese: 記録の上では《解決》している。 お得意のイカサマポーカーさ。
English: It's all "solved" on the record books. But it smells like a bad game of poker.

Japanese: しかし。オレはナットクできない。 ‥‥それだけのコト、だよ。
English: I can't let it go...
That's all there is to it.

Japanese: あの。どんな事件だったんですか？ 《ＳＬ９号事件》って。
English: What kind of case was it, anyway?

Japanese: 名前だけ、よく聞くんですけど‥‥ ダレも教えてくれないんです。
English: We've heard the name so many times, but no one tells us what actually happened.

Japanese: 知らないほうがいいコトも あるよ、バンビーナ。
English: There are some things you're better off not knowing, Bambina.

Japanese: それに‥‥あの事件は、２日前。 今度こそ、完全に死んじまった。
English: Anyway, that case is officially dead as of two days ago.

Japanese: “２日前”‥‥ 事件があった日、ですか？
English: Two days ago... the day of our case!

Japanese: そう‥‥ 《証拠品の申し送り》さ。
English: That's right... The evidence transferals.

Japanese: （御剣も言ってたな ‥‥《申し送り》）
English: (Edgeworth was talking about the transferals, too.)

Japanese: オレが知っているキカイは とりあえず、２つさ。
English: I know what maybe two of the machines in here do.

Japanese: と、“とりあえず”‥‥？
English: O-only two of them? There must be a dozen!

Japanese: 言っただろ、バンビーナ。 オレにとってキカイは‥‥
English: Like I said, bambina, Me and machines, well...

Japanese: ステーキのヨコの、ゆでた カリフラワーの次にニガテなのさ。
English: I like them about as much as I like stewed cauliflower with my steaks.

Japanese: まず。わかりやすいのは 《防犯カメラ》だろう。
English: The easiest ones to understand are these here security cameras.

Japanese: （ハラバイ巡査も言ってたな）
English: (Those are the ones that Officer Meekins mentioned.)

Japanese: 数時間ごとに、異常がなければ 消去されちまうらしいけどね。
English: If nothing happens, the tapes are automatically erased every few hours.

Japanese: そのカメラに、映ってたんですか？ 原灰巡査と‥‥多田敷捜査官が。
English: And Officer Meekins and Detective Goodman, are they on one of those tapes?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんね。
English: I reckon they might be.

Japanese: （セキニンシャが “たぶん”はないだろう‥‥）
English: (You're the security guard and you "reckon"!?)

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。
English: One more thing.

Japanese: 証拠保管室に入るときには、 ＩＤカードが必要なんだよ。
English: When you go into the evidence room... You need an ID card.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ドアのワキに カードリーダーがあるな）
English: (Thus the card reader by the door.)

Japanese: ＩＤカードを使うと、記録が残る。 ありがちだが‥‥わかりやすいさ。
English: The card reader leaves a record of every ID card passed through.

Japanese: （これが、ＩＤの使用記録‥‥）
English: (So this is the ID card record...)

Japanese: あ‥‥‥あれ！ 見たことがあるナンバーが‥‥
English: Hey! I've seen that somewhere before!

Japanese: おっと‥‥！　これ以上は 見せられないな、バンビーナ。
English: Sorry, Bambina. I can't show you more than that.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: コイツが事件にカンケイするか‥‥ オレは聞かされてないからね。
English: I haven't heard whether this is related to the case, yet.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　今の記録。 見おぼえのあるナンバーが‥‥
English: Mr. Wright! I saw a number on that record just now! I've seen that number before!

Japanese: （使用記録と事件との“カンケイ” ‥‥つきつけてやるんだ！）
English: (Maybe there's some way I can prove that record is tied to the stabbing.)

Japanese: すみませんけど‥‥そもそも、 《申し送り》って、なんですか？
English: Sorry, but could you explain what this whole "transferal" thing is about?

Japanese: 保管室には、解決した事件の 証拠品だけが眠っている。
English: We keep only evidence from solved cases in this room.

Japanese: それぞれの担当捜査官の管理で、 ２年間。ここに保管されるのさ。
English: They're kept here under the presiding detective's supervision for two years.

Japanese: 万が一、解決がまちがっていた場合 ‥‥再捜査のためにね。
English: So we can re-investigate them if it turns out there was a mistake, see?

Japanese: じゃあ、２年たった証拠品は どうなるんですか？
English: So, what happens to the evidence after two years?

Japanese: 警視庁の地下倉庫で、 永久に眠ることになる。
English: It goes to sleep forever in the underground vault at the Police Station.

Japanese: それが、《申し送り》ってヤツさ。 ‥‥年に一度、２月に行われる。
English: That's what we call "transferal." We do it every February.

Japanese: そう、だったんですか‥‥
English: I see now...

Japanese: 《申し送り》は‥‥そう。 事件の“葬式”とも言えるよ。
English: "Transferal" is like a funeral for old cases.

Japanese: 解決から、２年が経過したとき。 その事件は完全に終わって、死ぬ。
English: Two years after a case is solved... it's closed forever. Dead.

Japanese: もう、追跡捜査をすることは できない。‥‥永久に。
English: Never to be reopened again. Never to be reinvestigated.

Japanese: （そして‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》は ２日前、死んだワケか‥‥）
English: (And that happened to SL-9 two days ago...)

Japanese: ‥‥このニオイ。 テキサスを思い出すぜ。
English: That smell... Ah! Reminds me of Texas!

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥ テキサスのヒトだったんですか！
English: So, Officer Marshall... You're from Texas?

Japanese: こないだテレビで見たさ。 ‥‥ベイビイからの差し入れかい？
English: No, I just saw a special on television the other day. Is this from my baby?

Japanese: え、ええ。おキョウさんから‥‥
English: Uh, yeah, Ms. Starr...

Japanese: こ‥‥コイツは‥‥ッ！
English: Wh-what's this!?

Japanese: ど、どうしました？
English: What? What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥フィレ・ステーキ弁当‥‥！ そうか！　そういうコトかッ！
English: A filet steak lunch! I see... I see!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうイミがあるのかな、 あのお弁当。
English: I don't see. I wonder what it means?

Japanese: 罪門巡査に 《ステーキ弁当》をわたした。
English: Steak Lunch given to Officer Marshall.

Japanese: オーライ、バンビーナ。 アンタの勝ちだ。
English: Alright, bambina. You win.

Japanese: なんでもぐもぐもぐもぐもぐもぐ。 聞いてごくごくごくごくごくごく。
English: Ask mmph mmph munch munch Anything mmph munch smack.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥）
English: (Finally, it seems like...)

Japanese: ハナシをしてくれる気に なったみたいですね！
English: He's willing to talk!

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、テキサスの保安官は、 証拠品なんか信じなかった。
English: The sheriffs back in the wild west didn't place much faith in evidence.

Japanese: 信じたのは‥‥自分の ウデっぷしだけ、だったのさ。
English: About the only thing they trusted was their shooting hand.

Japanese: あの。ここは“ムカシ”じゃないし “テキサス”でもないですけど‥‥
English: Um... this is neither "Wild" nor "West" here...

Japanese: ハッ！　それとコレとは、 ハナシがちがうんじゃないかな？
English: Ah hah! But that and this are two different things entirely!

Japanese: ダメ。イミがわかりません。
English: I... guess so? Huh? I'm lost.

Japanese: （決定的にカンケイがある証拠品 以外、見せてもムダみたいだ）
English: (Looks like we need some evidence to get anywhere with this guy.)

Japanese: これ、見てください！ 被害者のＩＤカードなんですけど。
English: See this? This is the victim's ID card.

Japanese: ああ。‥‥たしか、現場の 地下駐車場に落ちていたな。
English: Ah, the one that was on the ground in the parking lot?
The number on this is...

Japanese: 罪門さん！　さっきの記録‥‥ ＩＤの使用記録を見せてください！
English: Officer Marshall! Show us the ID number on that ID card record!

Japanese: この、４番目のナンバー‥‥ カンゼンに一致します！
English: Look, the fourth number! It's a perfect match!

Japanese: 使用された時刻は、５時１４分。 事件が起こった時間‥‥だ！
English: It was used at... 5:14. Right before the stabbing!

Japanese: しかし！　ＩＤカードは、この世に １枚しか存在しないはずだぜ！
English: What's more, there's only one of them cards in the world!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥事件があった時間、 多田敷さんは証拠保管室に‥‥
English: So, when the incident occurred, Detective Goodman was in the evidence room!

Japanese: （でも、待てよ‥‥ 原灰巡査は、こう言っていた！）
English: (But wait, what did Officer Meekins say?)

Japanese: 保管室に入った本官は、ＩＤカード の提示を求めたであります！
English: Sir, I entered the evidence room, and asked the man to display his ID card!

Japanese: それで‥‥ＩＤの提示を求められた 多田敷捜査官は？　どうしました？
English: So you asked Detective Goodman to show his ID card. What did he do?

Japanese: なんとッ！　いきなり、本官に ナイフを向けたであります！
English: That's the thing! Suddenly he pointed a knife at me!

Japanese: （そのとき、彼がＩＤカードを 持っていたのならば‥‥）
English: (If he had his ID card then...)

Japanese: （原灰巡査に切りかかる必要 なんか、なかったハズだ‥‥）
English: (Why would he have pointed a knife at Officer Meekins?)

Japanese: ‥‥オーライ、カウボーイ。 アンタの勝ちさ。
English: Alright, compadre, you win.

Japanese: この記録‥‥アンタに わたしたほうがよさそうだ。
English: I guess I can give you this ID card record.

Japanese: 証拠品《ＩＤカード使用記録》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: ID Card Record added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （このリスト‥‥）
English: (I've got an idea...)

Japanese: （ＩＤを持っていそうな人物に 見せてみたほうがいいな）
English: (Maybe I should show this list to other people with IDs here...)

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 証拠保管室／第３号庫
English: February 23 Evidence Room Sector Three

Japanese: （静かだな‥‥ 捜査は終わってるみたいだ）
English: (It's quiet... the investigation must be over here.)

Japanese: 証拠保管室‥‥かあ。 ホント、お墓みたいですね。
English: So this is the evidence room? It really is kind of like a graveyard.

Japanese: “お墓”というより‥‥ “死体置き場”だよ。このムード。
English: Graveyards are supposed to have grass and trees. This feels more like a morgue.

Japanese: ここ、こ。怖がらせようとしても む。ムダですからね。
English: N-nice try, M-Mr. Wright. Y-you can't scare me!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: Eeeeeeek!

Japanese: ほほほおおおおおおおおおおいッ！
English: Whoooooah!

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさい。 悪霊かと思ったんです。
English: S-sorry, I thought you were a ghost!

Japanese: ‥‥悪霊に、いきなり ビンタはどうかと思うッス。
English: I wouldn't recommend going around smacking ghosts on the head, pal.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥ホントッスね？ さっきのハナシは！
English: So, is it true what I heard?

Japanese: ‥‥ボクさ。そんなね。 ケチなオトコじゃないワケ。
English: Wrighto, please, do I look like a selfish man?

Japanese: ダレにたのまれても、 ５０００円までなら貸すし。
English: Heck, if anyone asked me "sir, can I borrow $50?" I'd give them $50, no problem.

Japanese: ‥‥ぜんぜんモンダイないよ。 好きに調べてくれていいから。
English: So, go ahead! Investigate that room to your heart's desire! Knock yourself out!

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥ホントですよ。
English: Yeah, it's true.

Japanese: 巌徒局長殿が‥‥
English: So, Chief of Police Gant...

Japanese: ５０００円も貸してくれるッ！ この自分がたのんでもッ！
English: will loan anyone 50 bucks? Even me!?

Japanese: ‥‥そっちですか。
English: Oh, so that's what you were talking about...

Japanese: 今日は自分、捜査班長を 任命されていたッス。
English: Actually, I was put in charge of the investigation for today.

Japanese: １日だけですか。
English: Just for today?

Japanese: “１日班長さん”ですね。
English: Boss for a day...

Japanese: しかし、これからはッ！ アンタが１日班長ッス！
English: But guess what!? You got permission from the Chief... So now you're boss for a day!

Japanese: （うれしくないヒビキだな）
English: (Gee, thanks.)

Japanese: とりあえず、これ。 持っておくといいッス。
English: First of all, you'll want to have this.

Japanese: 証拠品《証拠保管室の上面図》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Evidence Room Floor Plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥おッ！ もう、こんな時間ッスか。
English: Hey, pal, look at the time!!

Japanese: あれ。何か用事でもあるんですか？
English: Was there something you needed to be going to?

Japanese: そろそろ、御剣検事の査問会が 終わるッス。
English: It's just that Mr. Edgeworth's inquiry committee should be letting out soon.

Japanese: 自分、今日の捜査の報告書を わたしてくるッス！
English: I'm going to go give them my report for the day.

Japanese: 何かの助けになるかも‥‥
English: It might help, you know...

Japanese: ほ、報告書、って‥‥ その、チラシですか？
English: R-report? You mean the note written on the back of that flyer?

Japanese: しかも、ウラに《異常なしッス》 しか、書いてありませんけど。
English: The one that says nothing but "No problems"!?

Japanese: 御剣検事なら！　こんな報告書すら 役に立ててくれるハズッス！
English: Hey, it's Mr. Edgeworth we're talking about! I'm sure he can use a report like this.

Japanese: 自分、信じてるッス！
English: I believe in him!

Japanese: （アツ苦しい信頼を寄せられる 立場も、キノドクなもんだな）
English: (Who needs enemies when you've got friends like Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃっ！ ひとっ走り、行ってくるッス！
English: I'm off, pal! Later!

Japanese: （御剣‥‥ぼくも、話を聞いて おいたほうがいいかな）
English: (I should probably see what Edgeworth has to say, too.)

Japanese: さっき入ってきたドア、ですね。
English: That's the door we just came in through.

Japanese: 出るときは、いらないみたいだ。 ‥‥ＩＤカード。
English: Looks like you don't need an ID card to get out.

Japanese: 罪門さんが電源を切ったら どうなっちゃうんでしょうね。
English: I wonder what would happen if Officer Marshall cut the power while we were inside?

Japanese: （そのへん、ちゃんと考えてる のかな、あのヒト）
English: (Let's hope he remembers we're in here.)

Japanese: うわ。おっきなガラクタが つみ上げられてますよ！
English: Wow! Look at this big pile of junk in the corner!

Japanese: これは‥‥クルマのドア、だね。
English: That looks like... a car door.

Japanese: ドアのフレームに 手錠がついてますよ。
English: There's a pair of handcuffs attached to the frame.

Japanese: ‥‥タイホした怪盗に 逃げられたのかな。
English: Maybe the guy they caught was some sort of escape artist and he got away.

Japanese: こっちはアレですね。 現場検証用のヒト型。
English: Hey, that's one of those human profiles for range testing.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒタイを キレイに撃ち抜かれてるな。
English: He's been shot square in the forehead.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんも、撃ち抜かれない ように、気をつけてくださいね。
English: Better him than us.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、ハミ出してるね。
English: There's something sticking out of here.

Japanese: シャツ‥‥かな。 何かの証拠品でしょうか。
English: Looks like a shirt. I guess it must be evidence for some case.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: I wonder if Detective Gumshoe put this here.

Japanese: ヒトを“だらしないヤツ”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kinda slob!

Japanese: 自分の保管庫は、そこじゃ ないッスから。‥‥とは言え。
English: I'm not responsible for the evidence here. That said...

Japanese: おそらく、これは‥‥最近。 開けられた保管庫ッスね。
English: I bet that evidence locker was opened recently.

Japanese: どうしてわかるんですか？
English: How do you know?

Japanese: こんなふうにしたら、 証拠品がイタむッスからね。
English: If you leave things hanging out like that, the evidence gets dirty or ripped.

Japanese: 見回りの巡査がチェックして、 担当の捜査官が注意されるッス。
English: The guard checks on that kind of stuff and notifies the detective responsible.

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、 うるさくてかなわないッス！
English: How many times have I had him breathing down my neck about some silly evidence...

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事さん、いつも 注意されてるみたいですね。
English: Sounds like Detective Gumshoe leaves evidence hanging out a lot, too.

Japanese: きっと、コートの下では、シャツが ズボンからハミ出てるんだろうね。
English: I bet he doesn't tuck in his shirt under that trench coat, either.

Japanese: ‥‥カゲグチなら、聞こえない トコでやってほしいッス。
English: If you're going to talk behind someone's back, don't do it right in front of them, pal!

Japanese: 白い布がハミ出てるね。 ‥‥シャツかな。
English: A piece of white cloth is sticking out... looks like a shirt.

Japanese: ちゃんとしまっておかないと シワになっちゃいますよ！
English: You'd better tidy it up or it will get wrinkled!

Japanese: ぼくをニラまないで くれるかな。
English: Hey, don't look at me! I didn't leave it hanging out like that!

Japanese: この中に、パンパンに詰まってる ワケですね。ユメと、証拠品が。
English: This place is stuffed with evidence... stuffed with dreams.

Japanese: ユメはどうかと思うけどね。
English: I'm not so sure about the dreams.

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Mmph!

Japanese: 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: そりゃ、そうだろうなあ。
English: I guess I should have known.

Japanese: まあ。最新式のセキュリティで 守られているッスからねえ。
English: Hey, pal, our security is high-tech around here!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、こまごまとした キカイが、ホコリをかぶってるね。
English: Some sort of bulky equipment is gathering dust here.

Japanese: かわいそうなほど ボロっちいツリザオがあります。
English: What a sorry looking fishing pole that is.

Japanese: あ、それ！　自分のッス。
English: Ah! That's my personal pole!

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 使ったことないッス。
English: I never did get around to using it...

Japanese: あれ！　これ、どこかで 見たような‥‥
English: Wait, I've seen that somewhere before...

Japanese: あ、それ！　金属探知機ッスよ！
English: Right, pal. That's that metal detector!

Japanese: ホレ。ひょうたん湖の事件を 解決にみちびいた‥‥
English: The one that led to the solving of that case out on Gourd Lake, remember?

Japanese: ああ！　なつかしいですね。
English: Oh, right! Wow, that feels like it was ages ago.

Japanese: そして、これは‥‥ 見たコトないですね。
English: And... hmm, I don't think I've seen this one before.

Japanese: あ、それ！　電波探知機ッス。
English: Oh, that! That's a radiolocator!

Japanese: 近い将来、なんらかの事件を 解決にみちびくかもしれないッス。
English: I'm sure it will come in handy in solving some case sooner or later.

Japanese: （このチャチなハコが‥‥？）
English: (That cheap-looking box?)

Japanese: 人間とキカイは、見た目じゃない！ ‥‥“マゴコロ”ッス！
English: You can't judge a person or a machine by their cover! You gotta look at their heart!

Japanese: うらぶれたガラクタたちが、 やつれ果て、打ちひしがれてます！
English: A pile of junk lies battered, worn and forsaken!

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、そこまで 言わなくてもいいんじゃないかな。
English: Aren't you being a little dramatic?

Japanese: ツリザオに、金属探知機。 これは‥‥知らないな。
English: Looks like a fishing pole... and a metal detector. Not sure what this one is.

Japanese: やるせなくホコリをかぶって、 ウツロな目でこっちを見てます！
English: They cast a withering gaze in our direction that screams, "dust me!"

Japanese: ‥‥だから、わざわざ文学的に 表現しなくていいよ。
English: Can't junk just be plain old junk?

Japanese: ここだけ、開いてる‥‥ インジケータがついたままです！
English: Look, this one's open! And there's a indicator tag stuck on it still!

Japanese: そこが、多田敷刑事の 証拠保管庫ッス。
English: That locker is coded with Detective Goodman's fingerprint.

Japanese: （被害者の‥‥保管庫！）
English: (Detective Goodman's locker!)

Japanese: いいんですか？　開けっぱなしで。
English: Are you sure it's okay to leave it open like that?

Japanese: 一度閉めちゃうと、 開けるのがタイヘンッスから。
English: Well, it'd be hard to get it open again if we closed it.

Japanese: （中身は、カラ。‥‥さすがに どこかへ移されたようだな‥‥）
English: (It's empty... they must have taken the contents elsewhere.)

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官の保管庫ですね。 中身はカラッポです。
English: Detective Goodman's evidence locker. It's empty.

Japanese: 保管庫を開けられるのは、 多田敷さん本人だけ‥‥
English: Only Detective Goodman himself can open this locker.

Japanese: つまり、このナイフを持ち出した のも、多田敷さん、ってコトかあ。
English: So, the person who took out this knife had to have been Detective Goodman.

Japanese: （なぜ、それがマフラーに包まれて マフラーから出てきたんだ？）
English: (And why was it wrapped in the "scarf" muffler stuck inside the "car" muffler?)

Japanese: こ‥‥これは‥‥血痕！
English: Wh-what's this...!? Blood!

Japanese: カスれてますけど、 アキラカに、手のアトですッ！
English: It's a little worn... but there's definitely a handprint here!

Japanese: ふき消そうとして 残ってしまったみたいだな‥‥
English: It looks like someone tried to wipe it off.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　この部屋‥‥
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 他にも、血痕が 残っているかもしれません！
English: What if there are other bloodstains left in the room!?

Japanese: （‥‥あのクスリを使って、 よく調べたほうがいいな）
English: (We should use her testing fluid to check it out.)

Japanese: こんなところに、手袋が ‥‥かたっぽだけ。
English: Someone left a glove here... but only one.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: Detective Gumshoe, maybe?

Japanese: ヒトを“うっかり屋”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kind of absent- minded detective!

Japanese: ソイツも‥‥ “例の事件”の証拠品ッスね。
English: That's evidence from "the" case, you know.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ですか‥‥ （たしかに、タグがついてるな）
English: You mean SL-9? (It does have a tag on it...)

Japanese: 証拠品《ゴム手袋》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Rubber Glove added to the Court Record.

Japanese: きのう、初めて見ました。 この、黄色いテープ。
English: That yellow crime scene tape! You know, yesterday was my first time seeing that!

Japanese: 群衆をかきわけて、これを くぐるのがユメだったんですけど。
English: It was always my dream to push through the crowd and just step over the tape!

Japanese: ２日連続で出くわすと、ちょっと ヘコんじゃいますね！
English: It's a little daunting to encounter it again so soon!

Japanese: （‥‥そうは見えないけど）
English: (I think maybe you're overreacting a little...)

Japanese: ‥‥ハデに割れたみたいですね。 何か、大きなものが。
English: Wow, someone must have broken something big to make all these pieces.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: Detective Gumshoe, perhaps?

Japanese: ヒトを“いたずらっ子”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kind of hooligan!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥“例の事件”の 証拠品みたいッスね。
English: That's apparently from "the" case.

Japanese: 例の事件‥‥？
English: "The" case?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ッス。 カケラの１枚に、小さなシールが。
English: The SL-9 Incident, pal. See the sticker on one of the pieces there?

Japanese: （《ＳＬ９号事件》の証拠品が また出てきたか‥‥）
English: (Another piece of SL-9 evidence...)

Japanese: （‥‥やめておこう。 時間がもったいない‥‥）
English: (Let's not waste too much time with this now.)

Japanese: あとで、ちゃんと 調べてみましょうね。
English: We can check it out later.

Japanese: このカケラを組み合わせると、 こんなツボになるワケだね。
English: If you put these pieces together, it makes a jar.

Japanese: ‥‥気になる点が、 ２つほどありますね。
English: There are two things that bother me.

Japanese: １つは‥‥ なぜ、カケラが足りないのか？
English: One: why are some of the pieces missing?

Japanese: そして、もう１つは‥‥ ちょっと不安定すぎます！
English: Two: doesn't it seem a little unstable!?

Japanese: そりゃ、割れますよ！
English: No wonder it broke!

Japanese: ‥‥今度、ツボを作ることが あったら、気をつけるよ。
English: I'll make sure to remember that next time I make a jar.

Japanese: なんでこんなところに、 ペンキとノコギリが‥‥
English: What is a saw and paint doing here?

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシから。
English: Since the dawn of time...

Japanese: ゲージュツはいつも、 《迫害》との戦いだったッス。
English: True art has always been a war against oppression.

Japanese: ゲージュツ？　そういえば‥‥ このペンキ、水色と黄色ですね。
English: True art...? I noticed that there's blue and yellow paint here.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ここで 生まれたんですか？　タイホくん。
English: Perhaps we're witnessing the birthplace of the Blue Badger?

Japanese: まあ‥‥自分にとっては、 アトリエ？　みたいなモノッスね。
English: Well... You might say this is my studio...

Japanese: （いいのか‥‥？）
English: (Here? In the evidence room!?)

Japanese: 青いペンキと黄色いペンキが ありますね。
English: There's blue and yellow paint here.

Japanese: これだけあれば、成歩堂さんの 絵、描けますよ、あたし。
English: This is all I'd need to paint a portrait of you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: いちおう、ネクタイも してるんだけどな、ぼく。
English: I am wearing a red necktie, you know...

Japanese: １日班長さん、なんですよね？ 刑事さん。
English: So, Detective Gumshoe, you're boss for the day?

Japanese: そッスよ！　メーヨな任務ッス！
English: That's right! It's an honor!

Japanese: なんせ、警察局内で発生した サツジン事件ッスからねえ‥‥
English: After all, the murder took place right here, in the Police Department!

Japanese: それにしては‥‥ ヒトリぽっちじゃないですか。
English: But, if you're boss... why are you all alone!?

Japanese: 他のヒトたちは‥‥？
English: Where are your underlings?

Japanese: きのうの捜査をもとに、明日の 法廷のジュンビをしているッス。
English: They're using our findings from yesterday's investigation to prepare for the trial.

Japanese: （‥‥つまり。今日もハズされて るのか。捜査班から‥‥）
English: (In other words, Detective Gumshoe got kicked out of the investigation again...)

Japanese: でも自分、メゲないッス！
English: I'm adamant, though!

Japanese: 担当捜査官の座をモギとって、 この事件を眠らせてみせるッス！
English: I'm going to take control and put this case to rest!

Japanese: ‥‥自分の証拠保管庫にッ！
English: And in my own evidence locker, pal!

Japanese: イトノコさんの保管庫も あるんですか？　ここに。
English: You have a locker in here, too, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ハッハッ。そりゃそッス。 自分は‥‥刑事、ッスからねえ。
English: Hah hah, of course! I am a detective, after all.

Japanese: 自分にしか開けられない保管庫を、 バッチリもらってるッス。
English: They gave me a locker that only I can open, pal!

Japanese: “自分にしか”開けられない‥‥？
English: "Only you can open"...?

Japanese: 自分は‥‥御剣検事を 信じているッス。何があっても。
English: I'll always believe in Mr. Edgeworth, no matter what happens.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥今、査問会に 出頭しているそうですね。
English: So... Mr. Edgeworth is with the inquiry committee now, right?

Japanese: 今日の法廷の失態について、 セキニンを追求されてるハズッス。
English: They're trying to figure out who's responsible for the mess-up in court today.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、ですか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: コイツは‥‥“ウンメイ” というヤツかもしれないッス。
English: I guess this is what you call "fate."

Japanese: 御剣検事は‥‥まだ。あの事件から 逃れるコトはできないッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth just can't get away from that case...

Japanese: “あの事件”‥‥？
English: That case...?

Japanese: あれ。聞いてないッスか？ ‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》ッスよ。
English: Yeah, that case! The SL-9 Incident, of course!

Japanese: 御剣検事にとっては‥‥ あれが“始まり”だったッス。
English: That was the beginning of the end for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: （もう少し、詳しく 聞き出せないかな‥‥）
English: (Maybe we can get him to tell us more about the case...)

Japanese: ここは、見た目以上に “ハイテク”ッスからねー。
English: This place is more high- tech than you might think.

Japanese: 保管庫は、それぞれの捜査官しか 開けられないようになってるッス。
English: Every locker is fixed so that only one detective can open it.

Japanese: この、ＩＤカードを使うんですか？
English: Using this ID card?

Japanese: ＩＤカードは、なくすことが あるッスからねー。
English: Well, that's the thing, pal. ID cards can be lost.

Japanese: 自分、今年に入ってから もう３枚目ッス。
English: Why, I'm on my third card since entering the force already.

Japanese: 落としすぎです。
English: That sounds like a lot.

Japanese: ま。そんな自分でも、右手を 落とすことはできないッスから。
English: Yes, but even I can't lose my own right hand!

Japanese: “みぎて”‥‥あ！ “指紋”‥‥ですか？
English: "Right hand"...? Oh! You mean, your fingerprint?

Japanese: そッス！　保管庫は、指紋による ロックで守られているッス！
English: Exactly, pal! The lock for each locker is coded with a fingerprint!

Japanese: 自分が開けられるのは、 あの保管庫だけッス。
English: And that's the only locker we can open.

Japanese: でも‥‥そんなスゴいシカケが あるようには見えませんね。
English: Funny, they look like normal lockers...

Japanese: さすが、最新型ッス。‥‥なんと！ 取っ手にヒミツがあるッス！
English: These are the latest model! There's a trick to the handles, see?

Japanese: とって‥‥
English: The handles?

Japanese: ウラに、センサーがついてるッス。 登録されていない指紋だと‥‥
English: On the other side of the handles is a sensor, and if the wrong person touches it...

Japanese: 電気ショック！　ですね。
English: Bzzzap! You get a shock!

Japanese: そんなコトされたら自分、 毎日が黒こげッス。
English: If that's what happened, my hand would be black and smoking every day!

Japanese: とにかく、パッと見た感じが あまりに自然なシカケなので‥‥
English: In any case, the locks aren't that obvious.

Japanese: 捜査官の中には、指紋のロックを 知らない連中もいるみたいッスね。
English: There's even some people in the force that don't know about the fingerprint locks!

Japanese: 証拠品《証拠保管庫》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Evidence Locker added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥かなり、 凶悪な事件だったらしいッス。
English: Now that was a bloody, violent case.

Japanese: 凶悪‥‥というと、 殺人事件ですか？
English: Violent... so it was a murder?

Japanese: 連続殺人事件ッス。
English: A serial killing!

Japanese: （“連続”‥‥扱いたくないな、 そんなオソロシイ事件‥‥）
English: (A serial killing... Maybe I don't want to get involved in this after all.)

Japanese: ハンニンはミスを犯して、 御剣検事が立件に成功したッス。
English: But the killer made a mistake, and Mr. Edgeworth built his case around that to nab him.

Japanese: それが‥‥今から ２年前のコトだったんですね。
English: And this was two years ago?

Japanese: 御剣検事は名を上げて‥‥ 同時に、黒い疑惑に包まれたッス。
English: That put Mr. Edgeworth right in the spotlight... and started the rumor-mill.

Japanese: （証拠品の“ねつ造”か‥‥）
English: Rumors... (About forged evidence?)

Japanese: おとといの《申し送り》で、 完全にカタがつくはずだったッス。
English: It was supposed to be all cleaned up with the "transferal" the other day.

Japanese: ケッキョク‥‥それが、最後の シゴトになっちまったッスね。
English: It was the last job he ever did...

Japanese: ‥‥多田敷刑事の。
English: Detective Goodman, that is.

Japanese: え‥‥ どういうコトですか？
English: Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》の担当捜査官は、 被害者の多田敷　道夫だったッス。
English: Detective Goodman was the detective in charge of the SL-9 Incident, see.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥あの、 飛び出しナイフは‥‥
English: So, so... That switchblade knife...

Japanese: 被害者自身が、証拠保管庫から 取りだしたもの、なのか‥‥？
English: The victim took the knife out of the evidence locker himself?

Japanese: 刑事さん。ほら、これ。 弁護士がつけるバッジですよ。
English: Detective... here's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、会うたびに 見せるッスね、それ。
English: You show that to me every time we meet, pal.

Japanese: 男はだまって警察手帳ッス！
English: Real men show their police badge. 'Nuff said!

Japanese: いいなあ。あたしも 早くほしいな、身分証明‥‥
English: I wish I had a badge... Even an ID card would be nice...

Japanese: 事件の被害者・多田敷刑事の ＩＤカードッス。
English: That's the victim, Detective Goodman's ID card.

Japanese: 今日び、なんでもカードに暗証番号 ‥‥気が休まるヒマがないッス。
English: These days, everything's cards and secret numbers. I can never relax!

Japanese: それは、イトノコ刑事が ぽろぽろ落とすからですよ。
English: That's only because you always lose your card.

Japanese: 暗証番号もぽろぽろ忘れるッス。 ‥‥自分がコワくなるッス。
English: I always forget my secret number, too. Scary, huh?

Japanese: ダレが見たって、自分は自分！ それでいいじゃないッスかッ！
English: My face should be ID enough! What's the world coming to!?

Japanese: （開きなおった‥‥）
English: (Detective Gumshoe, rebel against the system.)

Japanese: 御剣検事のクルマに、 御剣検事のナイフ‥‥
English: Found in Mr. Edgeworth's car, stabbed with Mr. Edgeworth's knife, huh...

Japanese: どうして宝月主席検事殿は、 そんなコトをしたッスかねえ‥‥
English: What would drive Chief Prosecutor Skye to do such a thing?

Japanese: あ。あやや。も、モチロン！ ハンニンは、ベツのニンゲンッス！
English: W-wait, I didn't mean... I mean, sure, of course someone else really did it!

Japanese: そのハンニンは、まるで‥‥
English: Someone who must have, um...

Japanese: 御剣検事に、ウラミでも あるみたいッスねえ‥‥
English: Someone who must have a grudge against Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: お、それ！
English: Hey, that's it!

Japanese: 御剣検事がもらった 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》ッス！
English: That's the "King of Prosecutors" award that Mr. Edgeworth got recently!

Japanese: 授賞式、イトノコ刑事も 出席していたんですか？
English: Were you at the awards ceremony, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: そりゃそッス！ 自分ももらったッスよ。努力賞。
English: Of course, pal! I got an award for diligence, myself.

Japanese: はあ‥‥おめでとうございます。
English: Ah... congratulations.

Japanese: それにしても、 なんで《盾》なんですか？
English: I was wondering, why is the award a shield?

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、割れてるし。
English: And... why is it broken?

Japanese: それはモチロン、 ちゃーんと理由があるッス。
English: Oh, there's a reason.

Japanese: ええと‥‥‥‥‥‥ また、今度教えてあげるッス。
English: Erm... I'll tell you what it is later.

Japanese: （忘れたらしいな、どうやら）
English: (Apparently, he's forgotten.)

Japanese: この写真を、おキョウさんが‥‥
English: That's the photo that Ms. Starr took.

Japanese: 何か、ココロ当たりありますか？
English: Anything you can tell us about it?

Japanese: スゴいヒトッス。おキョウさんは。 ‥‥この自分、１６才の冬‥‥
English: That Ms. Starr is quite the lady.
Why, I remember it was winter... I was 16.

Japanese: あの事件をしゃべらされたのは、 あのヒトが最初で‥‥最後ッス。
English: She was the only one who ever got me to talk about what happened.

Japanese: １６才‥‥あたしと同い年だ。 何があったんだろ。
English: 16... that's how old I am now! I wonder what happened?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事も、 ムカシは高校生だったんだな）
English: (I wonder if Detective Gumshoe wore a trench coat in high school, too.)

Japanese: あるイミ‥‥自分の代表作、ッス。 せいいっぱい、やってみたッス。
English: This is my crowning achievement, my masterpiece, you might say.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥ゲージュツは やっぱり、リカイされにくいッス。
English: But... art is always misunderstood, pal.

Japanese: げーじゅつ‥‥
English: Art...?

Japanese: 授賞式当日も、彼はホコらしげに おどっていたッスが‥‥
English: He was dancing proudly on the day of the awards ceremony...

Japanese: 式典が終わって、関係者の 出入りが多くなって‥‥
English: But there were a lot of people coming and going after the ceremony...

Japanese: 一時的に、どこかに 持っていかれちゃったッス。
English: So they took the Blue Badger away for a while!

Japanese: あれ。そうなんですか。
English: Really? Why?

Japanese: ‥‥“みっともない”から。 連中、そんなコト言ってたッス。
English: Oh, they said it was "shameful" or something like that.

Japanese: “みっともない”ッスと！ 自分が、いっしょうけんめい‥‥
English: "Shameful"! I toiled night and day...

Japanese: （まあ‥‥ あるイミ、しかたないよな）
English: (I sympathize with Detective Gumshoe... but I can see why they moved it.)

Japanese: 指紋が一致しないかぎり、 開けることはできないッス。
English: You can't open the lockers if your fingerprint doesn't match.

Japanese: 開けることができたら‥‥ ５０円！　払うッス。
English: If you can open it... they'll give you 50 cents!

Japanese: ‥‥警察にも、ゼッタイの 自信はないもよう、と。
English: Note: the Police Department lacks faith in its lock system.

Japanese: 現に、事件が起こったとき、 保管庫を開けた多田敷刑事が‥‥
English: After all, Detective Goodman was stabbed here after opening his locker...

Japanese: 同じ時間に、検事局で 死体になったワケッスからねえ。
English: But at the same time he was found dead over at the Prosecutor's Office!

Japanese: これ‥‥見ていただけますか。
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: 事件当日、この現場に入った ヒトのＩＤなんですけど。
English: This is the ID card record of the people who came in here on the day of the stabbing.

Japanese: ああ。ウワサは聞いてるッス。
English: Ah, I heard the rumors.

Japanese: 事件の時間に保管室に入ったのは、 やっぱり多田敷刑事だった‥‥
English: So, it was Goodman who came in here at the time of the murder...

Japanese: ああああああああああああああッ！
English: Whoooooooooah!!!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか‥‥？
English: Wh-what is it?

Japanese: こ、こ、こ、こ、こ、こ、こ、こ。 この、２番目の番号‥‥
English: Th-th-th-th-th-th! That second number...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ イトノコ刑事のＩＤ、ですか‥‥？
English: It's not your ID number is it, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ‥‥みつるぎけんじ‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth!
What...?

Japanese: この、２番目の番号は‥‥ 御剣検事のものッスゥゥゥッ！
English: The second number on this list... belongs to Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: え‥‥‥えええええええええッ！
English: What... Whaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: 証拠品《ＩＤカード使用記録》に データを追加した。
English: ID Card Record updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: （御剣が‥‥なぜ、保管室に？）
English: (Why would Edgeworth have come to the evidence room!?)

Japanese: 多田敷刑事のメモと、 この飛び出しナイフ‥‥
English: Detective Goodman's note... and that switchblade knife.

Japanese: きっと、イチバン驚いたのは 御剣検事だったッス。
English: I bet Edgeworth was the most surprised of anyone.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ですか。
English: Because of the SL-9 connection?

Japanese: ‥‥２年前。御剣検事が 初めて扱った、大きな事件ッス。
English: That was Mr. Edgeworth's first big case you know, two years ago.

Japanese: 検事の実力を、世間に見せつけた 最初の事件だったッスよ。
English: That was the first time the world knew Edgeworth was a man to be feared!

Japanese: でも‥‥その証拠品が、 なぜ、今になって‥‥？
English: But, why would evidence from that case turn up now?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥まだ。
English: I guess it's not over, pal.

Japanese: あの事件は、カタがついてない。 そういうコトッスかねえ‥‥
English: Maybe there are some loose ends left on that case...

Japanese: イッピキの刑事として、 これだけは断言しておくッス！
English: Allow me to say one thing, speaking as a detective.

Japanese: ココロ当たりのないモノには、 断固！　チンモクを守るッス！
English: If I see a piece of evidence I know nothing about, I say nothing! Nothing!

Japanese: か、かまいませんよ、それで。
English: That's... fine.

Japanese: ‥‥ホッ。
English: You bet it's fine, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥このカケラ‥‥もともとは、 どんなカタチだったのかな。
English: I wonder what shape these pieces were in before whatever it was broke?

Japanese: ちょっと、試してみましょうか。 ジッサイに作ってみて。
English: You want to try to put it back together?

Japanese: ‥‥ハッ！ シロートには、ムリッス。
English: Hah! Good luck, pal. That's no job for amateurs.

Japanese: なんせ、この自分が３時間かけても 歯が立たなかったッスからね！
English: Why, I spent a good three hours on that before I had to give up!

Japanese: こんなこともあると思って、 ノリを持ち歩いてますからね！
English: That's why I always carry around a tube of glue!

Japanese: （どうやら、これが“底”だ。 ‥‥組み立ててみよう！）
English: (Well, this piece looks like the bottom. Let's try putting the rest in place!)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ！　どう考えても、 カケラが合わないよ！
English: It's no use. These pieces just don't add up to anything!

Japanese: もしかしたら、カケラが 足りないのかもしれませんね。
English: Maybe some of the pieces are missing?

Japanese: とにかく、できるところまで やってみましょうよ！
English: Let's just try to put as many of the pieces in place as we can!

Japanese: （‥‥また、あとで がんばってみようかな）
English: (Hmm... maybe I'll give it another shot later.)

Japanese: あ、あれ‥‥‥
English: H-huh?

Japanese: いちおう、できたみたいだけど‥‥ 足りないみたいですね。カケラ。
English: Well, I think we did it. But some of the pieces are missing.

Japanese: 自分も、そこまでなら２分ッス。 モンダイは、そこからッスねえ‥‥
English: That only took me two minutes to do! The problem is finishing it!

Japanese: ‥‥カケラ、 盗まれたんでしょうか？
English: Were some pieces stolen?

Japanese: それとも‥‥ 最初から、足りなかったのかな。
English: I bet they were missing to begin with.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥
English: Still...

Japanese: 見ていて不安になるほど 不安定なツボ、ッスねえ。
English: It doesn't look like the most stable kind of jar.

Japanese: 割れてトーゼン！　みたいな ふてぶてしさを感じます。
English: I kind of understand how it got broken.

Japanese: 証拠品《不安定なツボ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Unstable Jar added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ こ。こんなところに、反応が！
English: Hey! A reaction!

Japanese: 犯人がフツウに逃げたとしたら‥‥ こんなところ、さわるハズがない。
English: If the criminal was running away normally, you wouldn't think they'd touch here...

Japanese: （何か、重要な手がかりになる ‥‥そう考えるべきだ！）
English: (This could be a big clue!)

Japanese: あ、アンタ‥‥ すげェクスリ、持ってるッス！
English: Hey, pal... Wow! That's some stuff you got there!

Japanese: あ、これですか？
English: Oh, this?

Japanese: 《ルミノール試薬》って いうんですよ！
English: It's called Luminol Testing Fluid!

Japanese: どこでッ！　いったい、 どこで売ってるッスかッ！
English: Where do they sell that?? Where!?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 自分も買うッス！
English: I want to buy some, too!

Japanese: 局長に５０００円借りて、 買ってくるッス！
English: I'll go borrow $50 from the Chief and buy some now!

Japanese: どこで売ってるの？　これ。
English: So, where do they sell it?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。あたし、いつも 通信販売で買ってますから。
English: Well, I always order mine through magazine offers...

Japanese: （とりあえず、上面図に 書きこんでおこう‥‥！）
English: (Let's mark this spot on the floor plans, shall we?)

Japanese: あ‥‥、やっぱり！ これって右手のアト、ですね！
English: Ah hah! Look!
It's a handprint! Someone's right hand touched here!

Japanese: なんッスとほほおおおおおおおッ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: What?

Japanese: コレ‥‥この、保管庫。 よりによって、自分のッス！
English: This... this locker! This is mine!

Japanese: え。刑事さんの‥‥？
English: This is your evidence locker, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: あ、アンタ！　自分が‥‥ 自分がタイホされたときは！
English: Hey, pal!When they... When they arrest me I want you to do me a favor, okay?

Japanese: ちゃんと証言してほしいッス！ “ニクめないヤツだった”‥‥と！
English: I want you to testify for me, pal! Tell 'em I was the kind of guy everyone liked!

Japanese: （‥‥これは、重要な手がかりだ！ 上面図に書きこんでおこう‥‥）
English: (This is a major clue! Let's mark the position on our floor plans.)

Japanese: ケーサツは信用できないッス！ ヨロシク証言、たのんだッスよ！
English: You can't trust those police! You'll testify for me, right?

Japanese: 刑事さんが言わないでください！
English: But, you're a police officer, too, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: かなり出血があったみたいだ。 （こんなの、初めて見るぞ‥‥）
English: Someone lost a lot of blood here. (I've never seen anything like this!)

Japanese: どう思いますか？　イトノコ刑事。 ぼく、シロートですから‥‥
English: What's your opinion, Detective Gumshoe? I'm kind of an amateur in this field...

Japanese: うーム‥‥‥‥ 青白い血‥‥ッスか。
English: Well... The blood does look a little bluish.

Japanese: 多田敷刑事は、もしかしたら‥‥ 宇宙人、だったッスかねえ。
English: Maybe, just maybe... Detective Goodman was an alien!?

Japanese: でも、やっぱり‥‥この保管庫で 事件は起こったんですね。
English: Whatever the case may be, it does look like the murder happened here.

Japanese: いちおう、上面図に 書きこんでおこうか。
English: Let's mark it on the floor plans.

Japanese: 自分のスイリは ほったらかしッスかッ！
English: So that's it, huh? You're just going to ignore my deductions!?

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 証拠保管室／第３号庫
English: February 23 Evidence Room Sector Three

Japanese: （さっきの調査で‥‥気になる “手のアト”があった）
English: (Our investigation turned up a suspicious handprint.)

Japanese: この、刑事さんの保管庫に 付着した、血痕‥‥ですね。
English: Here, in this blood on the detective's evidence locker.

Japanese: アイツから借りた、新兵器 ‥‥試してみよう！
English: Let's use the secret weapon we just borrowed!

Japanese: さあ、始めましょう！ まず‥‥指を選んでください。
English: Right. Let's get started! First... choose a finger.

Japanese: ユビ‥‥？
English: A finger...?

Japanese: ５本それぞれ、指紋のつき方は ちがうハズですからね。
English: Each finger leaves behind a slightly different imprint.

Japanese: 指紋がキレイに残っていそうな 指を選びましょうよ！
English: So let's choose the finger that will have left behind the clearest print!

Japanese: ‥‥パッと見た感じ、 ちがいがわからないよ。
English: I really can't tell the difference at a glance...

Japanese: ぐずぐずモンクを言わないで、 さっさと１本、選ぶ選ぶ！
English: Quit procrastinating and choose a finger!

Japanese: よーし。じゃ、いよいよ 指紋の検出に移りますよ！
English: Okay. Now it's time to check for prints!

Japanese: ちょっと、あたしが やって見せてあげますね。
English: Let me show you how it's done.

Japanese: （なんか、イキイキしてきたぞ ‥‥あかねちゃん）
English: (Ema's starting to get that sparkle in her eyes...)

Japanese: まず、アルミの粉を よーく、ふりかけます。
English: First, we sprinkle the aluminum powder around.

Japanese: え！　ど、どうやって？
English: Huh? How do you do that?

Japanese: スクリーンに、トンって さわれば‥‥ホラ！
English: Just touch the screen... See?

Japanese: おお。‥‥ソレっぽいね、なんか。
English: Ah... it looks like that did the trick.

Japanese: アルミ粉は、指紋にカンゼンに 吸着するんです。
English: The aluminum powder adheres completely to the print.

Japanese: いい具合に、アルミ粉を トントンって、ふりかけたら‥‥
English: Once the powder is well spread...

Japanese: よぶんなコナを 吹きとばしちゃってください。
English: just blow away the excess.

Japanese: え！　ど、どうやって？
English: Huh? How do I do that?

Japanese: ジッサイに、 息を吹きかけるんですよ。
English: You just blow. With your breath.

Japanese: ふーっ、て。‥‥ホラね！
English: Imagine you're blowing out the candles on a birthday cake. See?

Japanese: おお。‥‥楽しそうだね！ （なんか、抵抗があるケド‥‥）
English: Wow... that looks like fun! (It might take some getting used to though...)

Japanese: 大丈夫。　ハナに入ったり しませんから。
English: It's fine! It won't go up your nose or anything.

Japanese: ほどよくコナをふりかける。 よけいなコナを吹きとばす。
English: You just pour the powder on thick, and blow away the extra!

Japanese: ‥‥指紋検出のキホンです！
English: Those are the basics of fingerprinting, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （やってみるしかないな、 コレばっかりは‥‥）
English: (I guess I'd better give it a try...)

Japanese: （‥‥他の指紋を試してみたいな）
English: (I think I'd like to try one of the other fingerprints.)

Japanese: じゃあ、ベツの“指”に 変えてみましょうか！
English: Okay, let's move on to a different finger!

Japanese: あ！　やりました！ 検出できたみたいですね！
English: Aha! You did it! You found one!

Japanese: でも‥‥‥コレ。 どう見ても“指紋”じゃないよ。
English: But... this looks nothing like a fingerprint.

Japanese: う、うーん‥‥ たしかにそうですね。
English: Hmm... Now that you mention it, I guess it doesn't...

Japanese: どういうことかな。
English: What does it mean?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥‥ハズレだった、 というコトじゃないでしょうか。
English: It think it means... we're out of luck.

Japanese: ハズレ‥‥？
English: Out of luck...?

Japanese: この“手のアト”のヌシは、 手袋をしていた、ってコトですね。
English: The person who left this handprint must have worn gloves.

Japanese: ここまでやらせといて、 そりゃないだろう！
English: Don't tell me we've been wasting our time here!

Japanese: まま。こんなもんですよ、 カガク捜査なんて。
English: Hey, calm down. That's just the way it goes sometimes with scientific investigations.

Japanese: でも‥‥そうですね。
English: But... it does seem a shame.

Japanese: せっかくだから、 他の指紋を探してみましょうか。
English: While we're at it, why don't we look for other prints?

Japanese: 他の‥‥？
English: Other prints...?

Japanese: 保管庫のトビラを、もう一度 よーく見ると‥‥
English: Looking at the locker door again closely...

Japanese: “血のアト”以外の部分にも、 ユビの跡が残ってますよね？
English: it seems like there are fingerprints outside the bloody handprint as well.

Japanese: いちばんハッキリ残っている 指紋を検出してみましょう！
English: Let's see if we can find a clear print!

Japanese: （“血のアト”以外の、 ユビの跡、ねえ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... fingerprints outside the blood...)

Japanese: きゃあ！　ウットリするほどに ミゴトな指紋ですね！
English: Yay! A print so clear it's dazzling!

Japanese: う、うっとり‥‥？
English: D-dazzling...?

Japanese: それじゃ、セッカク検出した 指紋ですからね。
English: Anyway, this print took a lot of effort to find.

Japanese: さっそく、 《照合》してみましょう！
English: Let's match it up right away!

Japanese: まだ、あるんだ。 ‥‥いろいろ、タイヘンだなあ。
English: So we're not done yet? This is quite a process...

Japanese: まあ、セッカク 指紋を検出しても‥‥
English: Well, there's no point in finding a fingerprint...

Japanese: 持ち主がわからなくちゃ、 イミがないですからねー。
English: and not knowing who the owner is, right?

Japanese: （そりゃ、そうか‥‥）
English: (I guess she's right...)

Japanese: 御剣検事さんからもらった、 関係者の指紋データを見て‥‥
English: Look at the fingerprint data we got from Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 指紋のヌシだと思われるヒトを、 ズバリ！　指摘してください！
English: and point out the person you think left these prints!

Japanese: え‥‥！　そんなの、 わかるワケないじゃないか。
English: Huh? How am I supposed to know who it was?

Japanese: そんなコトないと思いますよ。
English: I could make a pretty good guess.

Japanese: “血のアト”と合わない以上、 この指紋は事件と無関係です。
English: The bloody handprint and the fingerprints are in different places right?

Japanese: ‥‥と、なれば。
English: That means that the prints probably don't have anything to do with our case.

Japanese: この保管庫に、イチバン指紋が 残りそうな人物は‥‥？
English: So, whose fingerprints would we most likely find on this evidence locker?

Japanese: （‥‥考えてみよう。 この指紋の持ち主は‥‥）
English: (Let me think... who could have left these prints...?)

Japanese: ‥‥一致しませんでしたね。
English: Hmm... no match.

Japanese: だって‥‥こんなの、 わかるハズがないよ。
English: C'mon! How am I supposed to guess whose they are?

Japanese: そうでしょうか‥‥
English: Isn't it obvious?

Japanese: 血痕と位置が合わない以上、 指紋は、事件と無関係ですよね？
English: The bloody handprint and the fingerprints are in different places right?

Japanese: だとすれば‥‥
English: That means that the prints probably don't have anything to do with our case.

Japanese: この指紋は、保管庫のヌシのモノで ある可能性が高いハズです。
English: They most likely belong to the owner of this evidence locker.

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか？
English: S-something wrong?

Japanese: そこまでわかってるのなら、 やってくれればいいのに。
English: I was just thinking, if you're so sure who it is, why don't you match the fingerprints?

Japanese: 成歩堂さんッ！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あたしは、これから先のコトを 思って、わざわざ‥‥
English: I was doing this for your benefit, so you'd learn!

Japanese: わわ、わかりました！
English: F-fine, fine!

Japanese: やっぱり！
English: Ah hah!

Japanese: この指紋、イトノコ刑事さんの ものだったんですね！
English: So these prints belonged to Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: “だからなんだよ”みたいな カオして。
English: You gave me this "so what?" look.

Japanese: まあ‥‥“だからなんだよ”って 思ってるからね、ジッサイ。
English: I guess that's probably because I was thinking "so what?"

Japanese: まあ。今回はカラぶりでしたけど、 次があるじゃないですか！
English: Okay, so we came up with nothing this time, but there's always next time!

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのトモダチでしょ？ “カラぶり”と“ドシャぶり”は。
English: Sometimes you hit, sometimes you miss!

Japanese: 気にしない気にしない！
English: You gotta roll with the punches, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、カドの立つ なぐさめかただなあ。
English: Thanks for the sympathy.

Japanese: （でも‥‥たしかに。 この部屋には、もう１つ‥‥）
English: (Wait... If I remember correctly...)

Japanese: （“手のアト”があった。 ‥‥調べてみよう！）
English: (there was one other handprint in this room. Let's check it out!)

Japanese: たしか‥‥ここに、 ルミノール反応があったよね。
English: This is where we got a luminol fluid reaction, right?

Japanese: あ、そうだ！ “手のアト”がありました！
English: Right! There was a handprint here!

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、 コレ、使ってみますか？
English: Okay! Want to try using this?

Japanese: （目つきが変わってる。 キケンだな‥‥）
English: (There go her eyes sparkling again...)

Japanese: ‥‥いや。ここはあえて やめておこうか。
English: Actually, let's not check.

Japanese: えー！　なんでですか！
English: What!? Why not!?

Japanese: あんまりジラすと、このコナ、 アタマからブチまけますからね！
English: Try my patience any longer and I'll dump this powder over your head!

Japanese: （‥‥早めにとったほうが いいかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... maybe I better check for those prints after all.)

Japanese: ‥‥よし。指紋を検出してみよう！
English: Okay, let's check for prints!

Japanese: そう来なくっちゃ！
English: That's the spirit!

Japanese: あ！　でも、今回は、 １つ注意しなくちゃ！
English: Oh! But I have to warn you about something first.
What?

Japanese: ここの血のアト、ふきとられて いたわけですよね？
English: The area with the blood was wiped away, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。クスリの反応で 見つけたワケだからね。
English: We only ended up finding it using chemical means.

Japanese: つまり‥‥その部分の指紋も、 ふき取られているワケです。
English: Any prints in that area will have been wiped away too.

Japanese: あ‥‥そうか。
English: Oh... right.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥“指紋はない”って コトじゃないか。
English: So... that means no prints.

Japanese: そのカクリツは、カガク的に考えて 高いと言わざるを得ませんか？
English: Would you say the probability of your hypothesis is high?

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくに聞くなよ！
English: D-don't ask me!

Japanese: とにかく。“ふき取られていない” 指紋を探しましょう！
English: Anyway... We must try to find prints that weren't wiped away.

Japanese: （“手のアト”以外に残された 指紋、か‥‥）
English: (Prints other than the ones left by the bloody hand...)

Japanese: こ‥‥この指紋‥‥
English: Hey, these fingerprints, they...

Japanese: だ、ダレですか！ あたしの知ってるヒトですか！
English: Whose are they? Whose!? Is it someone I know?
It's Officer Marshall!!!

Japanese: え。ざ、罪門さん‥‥？
English: Huh? O-Officer Jake Marshall!?

Japanese: 証拠品《罪門の指紋》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Marshall's Fingerprints added to the Court Record.

Japanese: そ、そんな！　グーゼンです！ 事件とはカンケイありません！
English: Th-that's got to be a coincidence! He's not involved in the crime!

Japanese: ‥‥あかねちゃん。
English: Ema.

Japanese: これは‥‥イトノコ刑事の指紋とは 決定的に、違うポイントがあるよ。
English: This are decidedly different from Detective Gumshoe's prints.

Japanese: ルミノール反応‥‥血のアトと、 指紋の位置が、一致している。
English: The luminol reaction. The blood and the fingerprints are in the same place.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああッ‥‥！
English: Oh... Oh!!!

Japanese: （ふきとられた、血のアト‥‥ そこに、罪門　恭介の指紋が！）
English: (So, we have Jake Marshall's fingerprints... on a wiped bloodstain!)

Japanese: 罪門さんが、どうして‥‥
English: But why would Officer Marshall...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥長かった調査にも、 やっと、コタエが出たみたいだ。
English: It looks like our investigation is finally turning up some results!

Japanese: こういうのを‥‥“決定的な証拠” っていうんじゃないのかな。
English: I guess this is what you'd call "decisive evidence"!

Japanese: そ、そんな！ 罪門さんが‥‥どうして‥‥‥
English: I... I don't believe it!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
